destinations.evidence: The dependency executor builder derives the verifier from BuilderContext, adapts it through SpcVerifierImpl, and passes Some(verifier) into the executor provider.
The pool-aware executor construction path uses the same BuilderContext-derived verifier and carries it into the RitualBlockExecutorProvider.

equivalence_argument: In the split architecture this behavior is owned by ritual-reth-nodebuilder-internal: both executor builder entry points adapt ctx.spc_verifier() into a trait object and pass Some(verifier) to the provider.
rationale: The primary diff removes local VerificationConfig, ExecutorRegistry, and ConcreteSpcVerifier construction, replacing them with ctx.spc_verifier().clone() before creating the SpcVerifierImpl passed to the executor provider. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When constructing the block executor provider, the node should wrap ctx.spc_verifier().clone() in SpcVerifierImpl and inject it into BasicBlockExecutorProvider::new_with_spc instead of creating a new ConcreteSpcVerifier with a separate executor registry.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct the node with a BuilderContext containing a verifier with a known registry entry or call counter, execute a block path that performs SPC verification, and assert the call observes the context verifier state. The test should fail if node construction creates a separate empty ConcreteSpcVerifier.
summary: Ritual executor construction uses the BuilderContext SPC verifier in the dependency repo.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Construct the node with a BuilderContext containing a verifier with a known registry entry or call counter, execute a block path that performs SPC verification, and assert the call observes the context verifier state. The test should fail if node construction creates a separate empty ConcreteSpcVerifier.
title: Block execution uses the BuilderContext SPC verifier
